title: Pentacalia moaensis
intro: Pentacalia moaensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia moaensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pentacalia moaensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Pentacalia moaensis is classified in the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of flowering plants.
